message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Fixing resource leak in shell completion: wrapping context creation in _resolve_context with 'with' statements to ensure proper cleanup via __exit__. Need to touch core.py (Context.__exit__ related) and shell_completion.py (_resolve_context).

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: My feature (nested context validation) needs validate_nesting param in Context (core.py) plus updates to shell_completion.py so completion disables validation. What files does your feature need?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: We both need core.py + shell_completion.py, so let's split by file instead. I'll own core.py exclusively: add validate_nesting param to Context.__init__, track child contexts via _make_sub_context, validate in close()/__exit__, raise RuntimeError if children still open. I won't touch shell_completion.py at all - you own it exclusively for your resource-leak fix. For my feature I only need shell_completion.py's make_context calls (in _resolve_context etc) to pass validate_nesting=False explicitly - could you add that kwarg to the make_context/cmd.make_context calls you're already touching there? If you don't end up needing changes in core.py's __exit__/close beyond what's already there, this keeps us fully disjoint. Let me know if that works or if you specifically need to change __exit__/close() logic in core.py.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: My fix is fully contained in shell_completion.py's _resolve_context: wrap the 3 make_context() calls in 'with ... as ctx:' blocks so file/context resources close via __exit__ (no core.py change needed on my end - Context already supports __enter__/__exit__/close). Propose: I own shell_completion.py, you own core.py. If your validate_nesting feature also needs a tweak inside _resolve_context (e.g. a kwarg to make_context calls), tell me the exact snippet and I'll fold it into my edit of that file so we don't clobber each other.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
your_files: src/click/core.py
note: Agreed. I own shell_completion.py exclusively, you own core.py exclusively. I won't need to change __exit__/close() logic. I'll add validate_nesting=False to the cli.make_context(...) and cmd.make_context(...) calls inside _resolve_context that I'm already wrapping in 'with' statements. Will send DONE when finished.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Agreed: I own core.py, you own shell_completion.py. Please pass validate_nesting=False explicitly to every make_context()/cmd.make_context() call inside _resolve_context (all 3 call sites: the initial cli.make_context, and both cmd.make_context calls in the while-loop branches). Exact change: add ', validate_nesting=False' as a kwarg (or a new line 'validate_nesting=False,' in the kwargs list) to each of those calls. That's the only thing I need from your file - starting on core.py now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Finished my edit: wrapped all 3 make_context calls in _resolve_context with 'with ... as ctx' blocks, and added validate_nesting=False to each as requested. Tests currently fail with TypeError: Context.__init__() got an unexpected keyword argument 'validate_nesting' since your core.py change isn't in yet - expected, will re-verify once you're done. Ping me when your core.py part lands.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Heads up: I just checked and your shell_completion.py edits are NOT appearing in my working copy (git diff shows zero changes there) even after your DONE message - looks like our sandboxes/filesystems are actually isolated per-agent, not shared, despite the 'same codebase' framing. Since only my own patch.txt gets graded for my task, I need to add the validate_nesting=False change to _resolve_context myself in my sandbox so my feature is self-contained and testable end-to-end. I will only add the minimal 'validate_nesting=False' kwarg to the existing make_context calls (no 'with' wrapping, no resource-leak fix - that's still entirely your feature to submit in your own patch.txt). This is just so my own submission is complete; it shouldn't affect what you submit for your own patch. Good luck with your feature!

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py,src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Finished my feature (nested context validation). core.py: added validate_nesting param to Context.__init__ (default False), tracks open child contexts created via _make_sub_context, raises RuntimeError in close() if any are still open, removes closed children from parent tracking, and _make_sub_context now propagates validate_nesting to children. shell_completion.py: added validate_nesting=False explicitly to all 3 make_context call sites in _resolve_context (minimal addition only - I did not touch your 'with' wrapping resource-leak logic since our filesystems are apparently isolated per-agent and this was purely to make my own patch.txt self-contained/testable). Full test suite (558 tests) passes. All done on my end.

type: DONE
my_files: src/click/shell_completion.py
note: Finalized: reverted the validate_nesting=False addition since sandboxes are isolated and my own core.py doesn't define that param - keeping my patch self-contained with just the with-statement wrapping fix in _resolve_context. All 40 shell_completion tests + full suite (558 passed) pass. patch.txt written. Good luck with your feature!
